name: Coast Guard TRACEN Cape May
tagline: The Coast Guard's only enlisted boot camp, at the southern tip of the New Jersey shore.
_note: Coast Guard Training Center Cape May (NJ; the CG's only enlisted basic training). CONUS full template (BAH MHA NJ198 Cape May; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Population = recruits + company commanders (CG's drill instructors) + staff. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil / FORCECOM, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). New Jersey is TRICARE East. Beach/shore town. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 08204 (Cape May).

data.Location: Cape May, NJ (southern tip of the Jersey shore)
data.Role: The Coast Guard's only enlisted boot camp (basic training)
data.For families: Typically a company-commander or staff tour

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Training Center Cape May is where every enlisted Coast Guardsman begins, the service's only recruit basic training center. The base runs on the rhythm of recruit companies coming and going.

It's at the southern tip of New Jersey in the historic beach town of Cape May. For families, this is usually a tour as a company commander (the Coast Guard's equivalent of a drill instructor) or support staff.

sections.title: Shore-Town Living, With a Demanding Schedule

data.subtitle: This is often a company-commander or staff tour, which can be time-intensive. The main choice is which Cape May County town fits your commute and family, since recruits live on base.
intro: Family housing decisions here are for staff and company commanders (recruits are berthed on base). Cape May is a seasonal beach town: lovely and quieter off-season, busy and pricier in summer.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Cape May, Cape May Court House, and the surrounding county, with year-round communities a bit inland.

recommendations.advice: Cape May and Lower Township sit nearest the base for a short commute, important on a demanding schedule.
Cape May Court House and inland county towns are more affordable and less seasonal.
Some military housing is available; check with the housing office early.
Beaches, lighthouses, and Victorian charm are the upside; off-season is calm and cheaper.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point; access is busier around recruit graduations.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Cape May has some military family housing for staff and company commanders (recruits are berthed separately on base); many families also live off base in the county.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA NJ198 (Cape May) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Lower Cape May Regional and other Cape May County districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

source.label: Lower Cape May Regional School District (official)

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, TRACEN Cape May
data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and area hospitals

data.Exchange: Coast Guard Exchange (CGX) at Cape May
data.Commissary: On-base commissary / CGX grocery (confirm current hours)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and in a seasonal shore market like Cape May tour length and the summer/off-season swing both matter.
rent: Your tour is short or your schedule is demanding (company commander).
You want time to learn the county before committing.
You'd rather not own in a seasonal, tourism-driven market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
A year-round inland town fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a place and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Cape May's seasonal market and demanding staff schedules lead many to rent. If you buy, lean toward year-round inland towns. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive (book early in summer). You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on Cape May County and on-base options before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and New Jersey vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Lower Cape May Regional or another county district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; the base clinic serves the assigned population.

data.a: It's the Coast Guard's only enlisted boot camp, where every new Coast Guardsman does basic training. For families, it's usually a company-commander or staff tour.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Cape May is MHA NJ198.
Staff and company commanders live in military housing or around Cape May County (recruits are berthed on base). Inland towns are more year-round and affordable.
A historic Victorian beach town: quiet and cheaper off-season, busy and pricier in summer.
